Tutorial

Create Professional AI Videos with HeyGen — No Camera Required

Learn how to use HeyGen to create talking-head videos with AI avatars, translate content into 175+ languages, and scale your video marketing without a studio.

By SlashAI Editorial · March 17, 2026 ·heygen, video, ai avatars, content creation, marketing
Tool HeyGen
Difficulty Beginner
Time ⏱️ 25 minutes
📚 HeyGen Links: Website · Our Review ·Docs

You need video content — course lessons, social media clips, product demos — but you don’t have a studio, a camera, or the time to record yourself 47 times until it sounds right. HeyGen solves this. Write a script, pick an AI avatar, and get a polished talking-head video in minutes. You can even translate it into 175+ languages with your voice cloned and lips synced.

This tutorial walks you through creating your first AI video from scratch.

What You’ll Need

  • A HeyGen account (free tier available)
  • A script or talking points (we’ll help you write one)
  • 25 minutes
  • Optional: a product image or your own photo for a custom avatar

Step 1: Create Your HeyGen Account

  1. Go to heygen.com
  2. Click Get Started For Free
  3. Sign up with your Google account or email
  4. You’ll land in the HeyGen dashboard with 1 free credit to start

The free plan gives you enough to test the platform. You’ll need a Creator plan ($29/mo) for regular content production — but get comfortable on the free tier first.

Step 2: Choose Your Video Type

From the dashboard, you’ll see several creation options:

  • Text to Video — Write a script, get a full video with an AI avatar
  • Photo to Video — Upload a photo, animate it with your script
  • Video Translator — Upload an existing video, translate it to other languages
  • UGC Ads — Create product review–style social content

For this tutorial, we’ll use Text to Video — the most versatile option for solopreneurs.

Click Create Video → Text to Video to open the editor.

Step 3: Write Your Script

The script editor is a simple text box. Type or paste your script — HeyGen will have your avatar speak every word.

Tips for good AI video scripts:

  • Write conversationally. Read it out loud. If it sounds stiff, rewrite it.
  • Keep paragraphs short. One to two sentences per paragraph works best.
  • Use punctuation for pacing. Commas and periods control pauses naturally.
  • Aim for 150–200 words per minute of video.

Example script for a product intro:

Hey there. If you're a freelancer drowning in admin work, this might be the tool you've been looking for.

I've been using InvoiceFlow for three months, and here's what changed.

Instead of spending two hours every Friday on invoices, I now spend ten minutes. The AI categorizes expenses, generates professional invoices, and sends payment reminders automatically.

My clients pay faster, I stress less, and I actually get to enjoy my Fridays again.

Check the link below to start your free trial.

Write your own script, or use this as a template and swap in your own product or service.

Step 4: Choose Your Avatar

Click the avatar panel on the left side of the editor. You have three options:

Public Avatars

Browse the library of pre-made avatars. Filter by style (realistic, casual, professional), age, gender, and ethnicity. Click any avatar to preview how it looks.

Tip: Match the avatar to your brand tone. A casual tech product? Pick someone relaxed. B2B consulting? Go with a professional look.

Photo Avatar

Upload a single portrait photo and HeyGen animates it — facial expressions, lip-sync, and gestures. Great for creating a branded character or using your own headshot without recording video.

Digital Twin (Creator plan)

Record a short video of yourself, and HeyGen creates a realistic digital clone. This is the most personal option — your exact look and voice, without ever hitting record again.

For your first video, pick a Public Avatar. It’s fast, requires zero setup, and looks great.

Step 5: Select a Voice

With your avatar selected, choose a voice from the voice panel. HeyGen offers:

  • Multiple voice styles (conversational, energetic, calm, authoritative)
  • 175+ languages and accents
  • Adjustable speed and pitch

Preview voices by clicking the play button next to each one. Pick the voice that fits your content’s energy.

Pro tip: If you’re creating content in English, try the “Conversational” style voices — they sound the most natural and less like a robot reading a teleprompter.

Step 6: Customize the Background and Layout

HeyGen gives you several layout options:

  • Talking Head — Avatar centered, shoulders up. Best for direct-to-camera content.
  • With Media — Avatar in a corner, slides or images in the main area. Great for presentations and tutorials.
  • Full Screen — Avatar fills the entire frame. Maximum presence.

You can also:

  • Upload a custom background image
  • Choose a solid color or gradient
  • Add your logo as a watermark

For a product intro, try the With Media layout and upload a screenshot or product image.

Step 7: Preview and Generate

Click Preview to see a short preview of your video. Check for:

  • Lip-sync accuracy — Does the mouth movement match the words?
  • Pacing — Any rushed or awkward pauses? Adjust your script punctuation.
  • Voice tone — Does it match your brand?

When you’re happy, click Generate Video. Processing takes 2–5 minutes depending on length.

Your video downloads as an MP4 in 1080p (free tier) or 4K (Creator plan).

Step 8: Translate Your Video (Optional — Killer Feature)

Here’s where HeyGen gets powerful for solopreneurs. After creating your English video, you can translate it into any of 175+ languages:

  1. Open your finished video
  2. Click Translate
  3. Select target language(s)
  4. HeyGen clones your voice and re-syncs lip movements to match the new language

You now have the same video in Spanish, French, German, Japanese — whatever markets you serve. This is genuinely game-changing for freelancers with international clients or course creators who want global reach.

Step 9: Download and Publish

Download your video and publish it wherever you need:

  • YouTube / YouTube Shorts — Full videos or short clips
  • Instagram Reels / TikTok — Resize to 9:16 vertical
  • Website landing page — Embed as a product demo
  • Email campaigns — Link or embed in newsletters
  • Course platforms — Upload to Teachable, Kajabi, etc.

Real Use Cases for Solopreneurs

Here’s how freelancers and solopreneurs actually use HeyGen:

  • Course creators — Record lessons without being on camera for hours. Update sections by editing the script, not re-recording.
  • Product demos — Create walkthrough videos for SaaS tools or digital products. Update when features change.
  • Social media content — Batch-create a week of short-form videos in one sitting.
  • Client proposals — Send personalized video pitches instead of text emails. Stand out.
  • Multilingual marketing — Translate one video into 5 languages and reach 10x the audience.

Pricing Overview

PlanPriceVideos/moResolutionKey Features
Free$01 video1080pLimited avatars, watermark
Creator$29/mo15 videos4KCustom avatars, no watermark, translation
Business$99/mo60 videos4KTeam features, API access, priority rendering

The Creator plan at $29/mo is the sweet spot for most solopreneurs.

Summary

You now know how to:

  1. Create a HeyGen account and navigate the dashboard
  2. Write scripts that sound natural when spoken by AI avatars
  3. Choose and customize avatars, voices, and backgrounds
  4. Generate polished talking-head videos without a camera
  5. Translate videos into 175+ languages with voice cloning
  6. Publish across platforms

Next steps:

  • Create a Digital Twin by recording a short sample of yourself (Creator plan)
  • Batch-produce 5 social media clips using different avatars and scripts
  • Translate your best-performing video into your top 3 client languages

The goal isn’t to replace genuine on-camera presence entirely — it’s to scale your video output without scaling your time investment. Use HeyGen for volume content, save your real face time for high-touch client interactions.

A note on disclosure Some links in this article are affiliate links. If you sign up through them, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. We only recommend tools we've personally tested. See our full Affiliate Disclosure.
